ksa_text: Ability to translate analytical findings into actionable business recommendations, calibrating the level of certainty, supporting evidence, and detail to the decision authority and needs of the audience.

dcwf.top_element_label: Ability to develop or recommend analytic approaches or solutions to problems and situations for which information is incomplete or for which no precedent exists.

ddat.top_element_label: Applying statistical and analytical tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Applying statistical and analytical tools and techniques
Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Applied Analytics & BI KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.441) to justify inclusion for the Deputy CDO. Without stronger framework alignment, this ability KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain ability KSA be expected in real-world Deputy CDO roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm Applied Analytics & BI is not a standard competency expectation for Deputy CDO. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the Deputy CDO competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.441) and edge-domain placement (Applied Analytics & BI) do not meet inclusion threshold for the Deputy CDO. All passes support exclusion.
